versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "What is the outcome then, brothers? When you assemble, each one has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has a tongue, has an interpretation. All things must be done for edification."

context_lines: In **1 Corinthians 14**, Paul is correcting disorder in the gathered church. The whole chapter is governed by intelligibility, order, and edification, so individual instructions in the chapter must be read within that larger concern for building up the body.
explanation_lines: *This document is part of an ongoing biblical reference project. All content is subject to revision as study continues. Every believer is encouraged to examine these things in Scripture for themselves — "let all things be done for edification" (1 Corinthians 14:26).*
